violation_detail.note: Duplicate ' jumps' key (chosen token added twice) is deduplicated by JSON parser. The 3 alternatives after the chosen are NOT sorted descending.

chosen_token_first.all_positions: chosen token is always the first entry in top_logprobs dict

temperature_effect.temp_1.0: 2 violations per 30 positions (includes chosen-vs-alt ordering conflict at non-zero temp)

VAL-COMP-007.reason: Deterministic alternative sort order violation at position 14 (token=' jumps') with temp=0.0. The 3 alternatives after the chosen token are NOT sorted descending: '...' (-21.125) appears before ' jams' (-20.5625). Reproducible across 3 consecutive runs. All other positions (149/150) have correctly sorted alternatives. Chosen token is always first. The buildLegacyLogprobsJSON() code path is used (confirmed via raw JSON showing ordered string builder + sentinel replacement). Root cause appears to be in LogprobsCollector.capture() where alternatives.sort() produces incorrect order for this specific set of token logprobs.

frictions: Duplicate chosen token in raw JSON (lp.token:lp.logprob + topLogprobs[0]) creates redundant key pairs that are deduplicated by JSON parsers. While harmless for output, it wastes bytes.
